{{External|rs}}
{{otheruses|the quest|the statue|Giant Dwarf (statue)}}
{{Has quick guide}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = The Giant Dwarf
|number = 82
|image = [[File:The Giant Dwarf.png|300px]]
|release = [[31 May]] [[2005]]
|update = Keldagrim - The Dwarven City
|members = Yes
|series = [[Quests/Series#Red Axe|Red Axe]], #1
|developer = Vincent M
}}
'''The Giant Dwarf''' is the first quest in the [[Rise of the Red Axe]] quest series. It involves the dwarven capital [[Keldagrim]] and [[The Red Axe]] consortium who are in control of the city.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = The [[Keldagrim]] mines east of [[Rellekka]] and north of the [[Mountain Camp]]. Follow the dwarven statues to the underground river.
|startmap = 2842,10129
|difficulty = Intermediate
|description = Five hundred years have passed since the rule of King Alvis, saviour of Keldagrim and victim of his own inventions.

He founded the Consortium: the gathering of Mining companies that were to serve the monarchy. But, eventually, the monarchy came to serve the Consortium.

By the time of his death, the monarchy was all but abolished. Now the Kings in Keldagrim are but a distant memory... with only the statue of King Alvis to remind the people of the old days, the dark days.

So begins the Rise of the Red Axe, the new multi-part storyline centred around the dwarves and their capital of Keldagrim. The first quest, The Giant Dwarf, introduces the city and the powerful business Consortium that rules the dwarven realm. Discover weird goings-on, narrowly escape from falling chunks of rock, ride around in minecarts and... make sure you don't bump into the wrong person on the way to the city!
|length = Medium
|requirements = * {{SCP|Crafting|12|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|no}}
* {{SCP|Firemaking|16|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|no}}
* {{SCP|Magic|33|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|no}}
* {{SCP|Thieving|14|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|no}}
|items = * 200 [[coins]] for the clothes
* Means to cast [[Telekinetic Grab]] ([[air rune|air]] and [[law rune]])
* [[Logs]] (most logs will work, however, arctic pine, redwood & coloured logs do not work)
* [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Coal]] (obtainable during the quest with 30 [[Mining]])
* [[Iron bar]]
* 3 cut [[sapphire]]s 
* Various [[ores]] and [[bar]]s (mostly obtainable during the quest, see below)
* [[Redberry pie]]
|recommended = * Fast travel to several locations
** Keldagrim, east of Rellekka ([[Fairy rings]] to {{fairycode|dks}}, [[Enchanted Lyre]], [[Rellekka teleport]] or [[Teleport to House]] if your house is in Rellekka)
** Mudskipper Point ([[Fairy rings]] to {{fairycode|aiq}}, [[Rat Pits]] [[grouping]] teleport to Port Sarim, [[Rimmington teleport]] or [[Teleport to House]] if your house is in Rimmington)
** Varrock, if you have not yet met [[Thurgo]] in [[The Knight's Sword]] ([[Varrock teleport]], [[Chronicle]], [[ring of wealth]] to the Grand Exchange or [[combat bracelet]] to the Champions' Guild)
*[[Stamina potion|Stamina]] or [[energy potion]]s
*About 10 of each of the following items in your bank, which may or may not be necessary for the [[The Giant Dwarf#Joining the consortium|timed delivery tasks]];
**[[Clay]] (not [[soft clay]])
**[[Copper ore]]
**[[Tin ore]]
**[[Iron ore]]
**[[Silver ore]]
**[[Gold ore]]
**[[Mithril ore]]
**[[Coal]]
**[[Bronze bar]]
**[[Iron bar]]
**[[Silver bar]]
**[[Gold bar]]
**[[Steel bar]]
**[[Mithril bar]]
**Alternatively, you can gather all these materials, except clay, during the quest with {{SCP|Smithing|50|link=yes}}, and either about 5000 [[coins]] or a [[pickaxe]] and {{SCP|Mining|55|link=yes}}.
*[[H.A.M. Robes]] (Optional)
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Not completable, as it requires access to Misthalin to learn [[Thurgo]]'s location
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
[[File:The Giant Dwarf Crashing into the statue.png|thumb|220px|Crashing into the dwarf statue.]]
[[File:Dwarven Boatman chathead.png|left]]

===Property damage===
Enter the Keldagrim dungeon entrance, east of [[Relleka]] and just south-west of [[fairy ring]] {{Fairycode|dks}}. After entering, continue through another cave entrance directly to the east by the statues - do '''not''' head south, as that takes you to the base of [[Trollweiss Mountain]].

Talk to the [[Dwarven Boatman]] ('''not''' the [[Dwarven Ferryman]]), who will offer you a free ride to [[Keldagrim]]. A cutscene will play, during which the boat will crash into the statue of [[King Alvis]]. After being arrested and taken to the Black Guard headquarters, [[Commander Veldaban]] will tell the player that the statue was long overdue for replacement anyway. He wants you to help out the local sculptor to rebuild the statue.

:'''''You can now use the [[Keldagrim minecart system]] throughout Gielinor.'''''

Talk to [[Blasidar the sculptor]]. He lives in the eastern side of Keldagrim, across the bridge, south of the kebab shop. He will make you the statue if you can get him clothes, an axe and boots "fit for a king". It is advisable to complete the [[The Giant Dwarf#An axe fit for a king|axe part of the quest]] last, as [[Thurgo]] will teleport the player directly to Blasidar.

===Clothes fit for a king===
{{Needed|200 [[coins]], [[coal]], [[logs]] and a [[tinderbox]]|skills={{SCP|Firemaking|16}} {{Boostable|yes}} to light the spinning machine}}

:'''Note:''' A tinderbox can be purchased from the [[general store]] on the west side of town, and coal can be mined on the east side of town.''
{{Map|name=Vermundi|2887,10188|mapID=10|r=2|mtype=square|align=left|height=200|width=200|caption=Vermundi's location}}
{{Map|name=Librarian|2862,10225|mapID=10|r=5|mtype=square|align=right|height=200|width=200|caption=The librarian's location}}
Talk to [[Vermundi]], the silk trader in the corner of the east marketplace, and ask her about making the king's clothes. She can, but first she needs a picture of the clothes. Talk to the dwarf [[librarian]] in the [[Keldagrim Library|library]] across the [[Keldagrim]] bridge. He tells you to search the library for a book about dwarven clothes. Climb any bookcase with a ladder in front of it to find the [[book on costumes]].

:'''Note:''' If you weigh 30&nbsp;kg or more, you will be unable to climb the ladder. You can simply drop some items and then pick them back up.''

Give [[Vermundi]] the book. You now need to fix her steam-powered spinning machine. Use the coal on her machine with [[logs]] in your inventory, then use your tinderbox on it ({{SCP|Firemaking|16}}). If you fail to light it, just try again. Once the machine is lit, talk to her again and pay her 200 [[coins]] to get the [[exquisite clothes]].
{{Clear}}
===Boots fit for a king===
{{Needed|Means to cast [[Telekinetic Grab]] ([[Air rune|air]] and [[law rune]]).|skills={{SCP|Thieving|14}} {{Boostable|yes}} to steal the left boot, {{SCP|Magic|33}}  {{Boostable|yes}} to cast Telekinetic Grab}}

Go to the south-western [[anvil]] in [[Keldagrim]] (south of the Black Guards' HQ) and ask [[Saro]] about the boots. He sold them to [[Dromund]], another dwarf in Keldagrim. Speak to Dromund in his house just north of the bank, who will refuse to sell you the boots.

When Dromund is not looking towards you, steal the [[left boot]] from the table next to the door ({{SCP|Thieving|14|link=yes}}). The [[right boot]] is on another table, but you cannot steal it from inside the house because of his [[Dromund's cat|cat]] watching over it. Stand outside by the window with the right boot, then use [[Telekinetic Grab]] on it ({{SCP|Magic|33}}) when Dromund is not looking. Once both boots are obtained, they turn into [[Exquisite boots]].

===An axe fit for a king===
{{Needed|An [[iron bar]], 3 [[sapphire]]s, and a [[redberry pie]] (if you have not given [[Thurgo]] a free redberry pie in the past, with the required redberry pies for [[The Knight's Sword]] and [[Prying Times]] not counting).|skills={{SCP|Crafting|12}} {{Boostable|yes}} to attach sapphires to the hilt}}
{{Map|name=Santiri|2828,10231|height=200|width=200|rectX=11|rectY=9|zoom=2|mapID=10|mtype=rectangle|align=left|caption=Santiri's location}}
{{Map|name=Thurgo|3001,3144|height=200|width=250|r=2|mtype=square|align=right|caption=Thurgo's location}}
Talk to [[Santiri]], the tradesman of the [[Quality Weapons Shop|sword shop]] in north-west [[Keldagrim]]. He talks about how his family came to possess the [[Dwarven battleaxe]], mentioning it has been damaged and that the [[sapphire]]s on the hilt had either been stolen or washed away. During the dialogue where you offer to repair the axe, Santiri may note your mastery of [[Smithing]] but exclaims that even you aren't skilled enough. 

After he gives you the axe, use three sapphires on it to rebuild the hilt ({{SCP|Crafting|12}}). Talk to Santiri again, who says that only an [[Imcando dwarf]] could fully repair it.

If you have completed [[The Knight's Sword]], the player will remark that they know an Imcando dwarf. If you have not completed it, you will need to first talk to the dwarf [[librarian]] in the [[Keldagrim Library|library]] by the [[Keldagrim]] bridge. They will tell the player to speak to [[Reldo]] in the [[Varrock Palace]] library to discover [[Thurgo]]'s location; this is necessary to progress.

Thurgo resides in the single house at [[Mudskipper Point]], south-east of [[Rimmington]] and south of [[Port Sarim]]. The fastest way to get there is to travel using [[Fairy rings]] to {{Fairycode|AIQ}}. Go to him with an [[iron bar]], and ask him to repair the axe. After that, if you have not yet given him a [[redberry pie]] for '''free''' previously, you must give him one now. He will then repair the axe.

:'''''Note:''' Thurgo will supply you with another axe if lost, but it will require three sapphires to be added again.''

When Thurgo is done, you have the option to return immediately to Keldagrim. This will place you back in [[Blasidar the sculptor]]'s house.

===The Head===
Give [[Riki the sculptor's model|Riki]] the axe, clothes and boots by speaking to him with the items in your inventory. Talk to [[Blasidar the sculptor|Blasidar]], who explains that the Consortium wants the head of the statue modelled after one of its directors, but has not agreed which one. He asks you to go and help them make a decision. You will see a short cutscene of the incomplete statue.

:'''Make sure to complete this step before starting the tasks below, or the tasks for the director will go on indefinitely!'''

===Joining the consortium===
There are eight mining companies in the [[Consortium]], which are located upstairs in either the east or west marketplace:
*[[The Green Gemstone]]
*[[The White Chisel]]
*[[The Silver Cog]]
*[[The Brown Engine]]
*[[The Yellow Fortune]]
*[[The Blue Opal]]
*[[The Purple Pewter]]
*[[The Red Axe]] ''(you cannot join this company)''

You will need to pick a company to join. Joining [[The Purple Pewter]] provides a headstart of 20 points in the scoring system by speaking to the [[Purple Pewter Secretary]] whilst wearing three or more pieces of the [[H.A.M. robes]] set. Otherwise, your choice will only impact the [[Giant Dwarf (statue)|final model of the statue]] built at the end of the quest.

The companies on the west side of the building have faster bank access, whilst the east side is closer to the [[Ore seller.|ore seller]] and Grand Exchange (through minecart).

To begin, talk to the secretary of the company that you want to work for. You will be given several errands to collect 1-5 of a certain ''un-noted'' material within a time limit. The possible items are [[clay]], [[copper ore]], [[tin ore]], [[iron ore]], [[silver ore]], [[gold ore]], [[mithril ore]], and [[coal]]. With the exception of clay, all materials can be mined or bought nearby, if they are not already in your bank or inventory. You can say "no," then talk to the secretary again to receive a different task.

A hidden scoring system determines your progress with a company; each ore delivery completed gives 20 points, while failing or cancelling a task subtracts 2 points. '''Progress is not shared between companies, so only perform tasks for one of them.'''

:'''Note:''' If you fail a task and are unable to obtain a new one, logging out and logging back in should fix the issue.

Once you reach 75 points, you will be told that the director of the company wants to speak with you. At this point you may start doing errands for the director, who will request different items from you. These items include [[bronze bars]], [[iron bars]], [[steel bars]], [[silver bars]], [[gold bars]], and [[mithril bars]], and give 12 points.

When you have have obtained 100 points for a company, you can ask the director to officially join them, and they will agree and accept you as a "trusted associate". Next, tell them that [[Blasidar the sculptor|Blasidar]] sent you, and that you will vouch for them at the next Consortium meeting.

===Art by committee===
Return to [[Commander Veldaban]] (west of the bank) and tell him you will go to the meeting. You will see a cutscene of about 4 minutes. You can also skip the cutscene by refusing to go to the meeting; you can read a summary of it in the [[meeting notes]], received after completing the quest.

<gallery captionalign="center" widths="200">
File:The Giant Dwarf statue.png|The completed statue representing the director of the Green Gemstone or the Silver Cog.
File:Blue opal dwarf.png|The completed statue representing the director of the Blue Opal.
File:The Giant Dwarf Brown Engine Director Statue.png|The completed statue representing the director of the Brown Engine.
File:Yellow Fortune.png|The completed statue representing the director of the Yellow Fortune.
File:Consortium Meeting.png|Hreidmar storms out of the Consortium meeting.
</gallery>

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = The Giant Dwarf
|image = [[File:The Giant Dwarf reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 2
|rewards = 
*{{SCP|Mining|2,500}} [[Mining]] experience
*{{SCP|Smithing|2,500}} [[Smithing]] experience
*{{SCP|Crafting|2,500}} [[Crafting]] experience
*{{SCP|Magic|1,500}} [[Magic]] experience
*{{SCP|Thieving|1,500}} [[Thieving]] experience
*{{SCP|Firemaking|1,500}} [[Firemaking]] experience
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of The Giant Dwarf is required for the following:
*[[Another Slice of H.A.M.]]
*[[Forgettable Tale...]]

==Transcript==
{{hastranscript|Quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 9 November 2022
|update = Diversity & Inclusion Changes and Quest Speedrunning Updates
|change = The gender restrictions on joining the Brown Engine or Yellow Fortune company were lifted as part of the [[Diversity & Inclusion updates]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 
|update = 
|change = [[The Consortium (music track)|The Consortium]] [[music]] track now unlocks when the final meeting cutscene is skipped.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 6 February 2014
|update = Quick-prayers & Loot Broadcast
|change = The statue in The Giant Dwarf no longer has huge vertical gaps between its component parts.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
* The cutscene between the Consortium leaders at the end of the quest takes place in a static, non-instanced room; a glimpse of one of these rooms can be seen during normal gameplay from the third room of [[Pyramid Plunder]]. This is because at the time of the quest's development, Jagex was unable to create [[instance]]d areas for cutscenes, even though [[The Lost Tribe]], another quest released on the same day but went into development later than The Giant Dwarf, was the first use of an instance in the game.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1162261891537707008|date=16 August 2019|archiveurl=https://archive.fo/19tgw|archivedate=16 August 2019|quote=Sort of. Before developers could generate instanced copies of areas on demand, cutscenes had to take place in static copies of the map, and you're seeing one of those.}}{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1644084798091153411|date=16 August 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/96343|archivedate=8 April 2023|quote=Yeah, Giant Dwarf went into development considerably earlier, being a bigger project due to the city being created as part of it. By the time Lost Tribe went into development, instances were available to be used. Mod Vincent would otherwise have appreciated them!}}
** As a result, there are sixteen distinct cutscene rooms (four for each floor the game possesses) in which the player can be assigned to. If a seventeenth player attempts to enter a cutscene whilst all sixteen cutscene rooms are occupied, [[Commander Veldaban]] will tell the player that the Consortium either has "other important business to attend to", or that the meeting hasn't started yet. The exact conditions to what he says can be found in the [[Transcript:The Giant Dwarf#Talking to Commander Veldaban|quest's transcript]].
*There was a graphical glitch that appeared during the cutscenes that shows a closeup up the Giant Dwarf statue: the body appeared to be floating above the legs, and the head (once it had been added) was floating above the body.
*If you talk to Dromund's Cat while wearing a catspeak amulet, he mentions he used to be friends with the Red Axe cat.
*When telling the player to pay for the clothes, [[Vermundi]] asks for "200 credits." This is the only place in the game where [[coins]] are referred to as ''credits''.
*When asking Santiri about battleaxes, he offers you a blue or red one, saying "red is on the rise". This is a reference to the name of the quest series, Rise of the Red Axe.
*When speaking to Santiri about repairing the axe, his dialogue changes depending on your smithing level. For example, if the player has 99 Smithing. "The axe is beyond repair, even for a master such as you."
*Near the beginning of the meeting, Hreidmar interrupts the argument by saying 'This bickering is pointless'. This is likely a reference to Governor Tarkin in ''{{Wp|Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope}}''.
*Completing The Giant Dwarf quest before choosing the statue's head in the [[Enakhra's Lament]] quest unlocks additional dialogue with [[Lazim]].

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{The Giant Dwarf}}
{{Keldagrim}}
{{DEFAULTSORT|Giant Dwarf, The}}
